pattern: Kadane's scan keeps the best sum ending at the current index, restarting whenever carrying the prefix would only hurt, so the global best emerges in one pass.
hints: A running subarray is worth extending only while its accumulated sum stays positive.
At each element decide whether to extend the previous best-ending-here or start fresh from this element.
Keep cur = max(x, cur + x) as you scan and track the maximum cur seen; that maximum is the answer even when every value is negative.

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs with a strong positive middle run
single element is its own best subarray
all negative so the answer is the least-bad single element
all positives sum to the whole array
a big dip must not be carried through

stress.description: random signed values to exercise frequent restarts of the running sum
stress.note: balanced sign distribution forces many extend-vs-restart decisions
